Migrating to AWS delivers operational resilience (multi-AZ / multi-Region designs) and business agility (rapid provisioning and experimentation). Amazon.com retail discounts, marketing terms like "business excellence," and staff retention are not AWS Cloud value-framework benefits.
The Performance Efficiency pillar's design principles include "democratize advanced technologies" and "go global in minutes" — exactly the goal of adopting serverless to use new capabilities quickly.

Amazon CloudFront is the AWS CDN — it caches video content at edge locations worldwide so global users get low-latency playback. Auto Scaling adjusts compute, ELB distributes regional traffic, Kinesis Video Streams ingests live video.

An Availability Zone is one or more discrete data centers with redundant power, networking, and connectivity in an AWS Region. A Region is a geographic area of AZs; edge locations cache CloudFront content; Outposts is on-prem AWS hardware.
Reliability is the pillar that addresses recovering from disruptions and dynamically acquiring resources to meet demand — both stated outcomes in the prompt.
Configuration management is explicitly a shared control: AWS configures the infrastructure devices it manages, customers configure their own guest OSes, databases, and applications.

With Lambda, AWS handles the OS, CPU/memory provisioning, and infrastructure security. The customer's responsibilities are writing/updating the function code and securing what is inside that code (input validation, secrets handling, IAM scope).

Dedicated Hosts give a customer an entire physical server, providing physical isolation from other AWS customers. Reserved Instances, On-Demand, and No-Upfront RIs are pricing/commitment models, not isolation models.
Security and compliance are a shared responsibility: AWS owns security "of" the cloud and the customer owns security "in" the cloud.

Elasticity — acquiring resources when you need them and releasing them when you don't — is a foundational cloud design principle. Provisioning for peak or always picking the largest instance is the opposite of cloud-native design.

EC2 AMIs let you re-launch identical instances in another AZ or Region; EBS snapshots back up volumes and can be copied across Regions. RIs are pricing, Shield is DDoS protection, GuardDuty is threat detection — none are DR mechanisms.

AWS Cost Explorer visualizes historical spend by service over rolling time windows up to 12 months. Trusted Advisor flags optimization opportunities, Forecast is an ML service for forecasting, CloudWatch tracks operational metrics.
Enterprise Support customers have the AWS Concierge Support Team as their primary point of contact for billing and account-level questions.
Consolidated billing through AWS Organizations rolls all member accounts into a single payer account, producing one invoice and aggregating usage to reach tiered-pricing discounts sooner.
Spreading workloads across Availability Zones is the canonical implementation of the "Design for failure" principle — assume any one AZ can fail and the workload still serves users.

Physical security of AWS data centers — access control, environmental safeguards, hardware decommissioning — is AWS's responsibility under the shared model. IAM lifecycle, EBS encryption choice, and security group rules are the customer's.

AWS Lambda runs event-driven code on demand and bills only for the millisecond-level execution time. For short-running, event-triggered workloads it is the most operationally efficient and cost-effective compute option.
The AWS SDKs let applications connect to and deploy AWS services programmatically. The Management Console is a UI, Cloud9 is a browser IDE, CodePipeline is a CI/CD service.
IAM credentials belong to the customer — rotating access keys regularly is the customer's responsibility under the shared model. AWS does not automatically rotate user keys.

PCI compliance on AWS requires using services that are in scope for PCI DSS and then implementing PCI controls at the application layer. AWS will not enable PCI features on individual customer accounts.
Trusted Advisor's five check categories are Cost Optimization, Performance, Security, Fault Tolerance, and Service Limits. Of the options offered, Cost optimization and Performance match.

S3 storage class selection (Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Glacier classes, etc.) has by far the largest impact on data-lake cost. Inbound data transfer to S3 is free, bucket policies have no cost, and request fees are usually small relative to storage.

Savings Plans commit to a dollar-per-hour spend over 1 or 3 years for steep discounts vs. On-Demand — ideal for a workload that must run continuously for 12 months. Spot can be reclaimed (bad for must-always-be-available), Dedicated Hosts are pricier, On-Demand has no commitment but is full list price.

"Perform operations as code" is a stated design principle of the Operational Excellence pillar — using scripts and infrastructure-as-code to apply the same DevOps practices to operations that you use for application code.
With RDS, AWS manages the underlying OS, engine patches, backups, and hardware. The customer is responsible for network access (security groups), IAM permissions, and the data itself.
Customer data is always the customer's responsibility regardless of the service model. Physical security, OS patching of managed services, and server-side encryption mechanics are AWS's.
Cost allocation tags attach metadata (e.g., business unit, cost center) to AWS resources so spend can be sliced by tag in Cost Explorer and the Cost & Usage Report.

AWS Service Quotas is the central place to view and request increases for service limits across AWS services. Config tracks resource configuration; Service Catalog distributes approved products; Budgets tracks spend.
"Make frequent, small, reversible changes" is one of the design principles listed under Operational Excellence.

AWS CloudTrail records every API call, including the IAM principal, source IP, and timestamp — exactly what you need to identify who terminated an instance. CloudWatch is for metrics/logs; X-Ray traces app requests; IAM defines permissions but doesn't log usage.

All AWS customers (including Basic and Developer support) get the core Trusted Advisor checks (a limited set of security and service-limit checks). The full check suite is available only on Business and Enterprise Support.
Firewall (security group) configuration is the customer's responsibility — an example of security IN the cloud. Edge locations, physical security, and global infrastructure are AWS's responsibility.
AWS Artifact is the on-demand portal for compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP) and online agreements such as the BAA.
Changing the AWS Support plan is one of the tasks that require root user credentials. Billing viewing, EC2 lifecycle, and opening support cases can all be delegated to IAM users.

Service control policies (SCPs) are an AWS Organizations feature that defines the maximum permissions available to accounts in the organization. They do not manage Regions, AZs, or edge locations directly.

AWS Config records configuration changes over time and evaluates resources against rules — the right tool for audit of change management. Trusted Advisor offers best-practice checks; CloudWatch is metrics/logs; Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.
Multi-Region deployments primarily raise availability — surviving the loss of an entire Region. Durability speaks to data integrity, security is broader, coupling is about software design.
Patch management is explicitly a shared control: AWS patches the infrastructure for managed services, customers patch guest OSes on EC2 and their own applications.
A VPC is regional — its subnets can live in any Availability Zone within a single Region, but a VPC itself cannot span multiple Regions.

A Site-to-Site VPN has a Virtual Private Gateway on the AWS side and a Customer Gateway representing the on-prem device. Storage Gateway is for hybrid storage; NAT and Internet Gateways serve outbound/inbound VPC internet traffic.

Patch management and configuration management are both listed by AWS as shared controls. Physical/environmental controls are AWS-only; server hardware management is AWS; application security is customer-only.
Loose coupling — components that interact via well-defined interfaces and degrade independently — is a foundational cloud design best practice (and an AWS Well-Architected principle).

Elasticity is exactly the ability to scale resources up and down with demand while preserving performance. Decoupling is structural; design for failure is about reliability; "think parallel" is a complementary concept but not the main fit here.
Spanning multiple AZs is the canonical pattern for high availability — the workload survives the loss of any single AZ.

Inter-Region VPC peering connects two VPCs across Regions using the AWS backbone with no extra hardware. Client VPN is for end-user access; Direct Connect needs a physical circuit; VPC endpoints connect a VPC to AWS services.

With RDS the customer manages database connections (security groups, IAM authentication, app-side connection pools). AWS installs and patches the engine, replaces failed instances, and provides KMS encryption — the customer just opts in.
With Lambda the customer creates and versions function code. AWS owns the servers, OS, scaling decisions, and runtime updates.

Decommissioning physical drives and controlling physical access to data centers are both AWS-only duties. Security groups/NACLs and EC2 OS patching are customer duties. RDS OS patching is AWS — though that option is not asked here as a customer duty.
The Concierge Team is exclusive to Enterprise Support. Trusted Advisor is in every plan (limited at lower tiers); support cases are available from Developer up; Amazon Connect is unrelated.

Trusted Advisor's check categories are security best practices, cost optimization, performance, fault tolerance, and service limits. Application performance and compliance status are not Trusted Advisor categories.

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adds and removes instances automatically in response to demand, making the fleet elastic. Resource groups are organizational; lifecycle policies belong to S3/storage; an ALB distributes traffic but does not change capacity.
Security groups act as stateful, instance-level firewalls. Network ACLs are stateless and operate at the subnet level. A VPC is the network itself; an ENI is just a virtual network card.

REVIEW: the source PDF appears to mismatch this prompt with options about shared-responsibility categories. Of the options offered, Virtualization Management is the only AWS-responsibility item present — but none directly answers "makes EC2 more elastic." Treat with care during review.
Enterprise Support is the only tier that includes a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) as a unique advantage.
Elasticity is the ability to automatically adjust compute capacity (scale out and in) to maintain consistent performance as demand changes.

When modeling AWS workload TCO, the major direct AWS cost categories are Compute, Storage, and Data Transfer. Facilities, network infrastructure, and hardware lifecycle are on-premises costs you compare AWS against — not AWS-side cost components.

Cost Explorer breaks down spend by day, service, and linked account; AWS Budgets sets thresholds and sends notifications. AWS does not automatically terminate workloads on budget overrun, switch purchasing options, or move S3 objects between classes by itself.
IAM roles give EC2 temporary credentials via the instance profile — never hard-code keys or read them from a file. Public bucket policies expose the bucket to the world.

Horizontal scaling across multiple Availability Zones is the canonical pattern for high availability. Vertical scaling alone, Dedicated Instances, or compute-optimized families do not address AZ-level resilience.
Going from a 3–4 week deploy cycle to 2–3 days is the textbook example of agility — the ability to deploy faster as a benefit of AWS.
Among Dedicated Host reservation payment options, All-Upfront yields the largest discount. Partial-Upfront is next, then No-Upfront. Hourly On-Demand is full list price (no commitment).

Auto Scaling replaces unhealthy instances and adjusts to load; spreading across multiple AZs survives any single-AZ failure. NACLs don't health-check; AWS SMS migrates servers; "AWS points of presence" relate to CloudFront, not three-tier app availability.

AWS handles database engine patching (for managed services) and storage capacity planning at the infrastructure level. Schema design, access controls, and application code remain customer responsibilities.

Trusted Advisor's core checks include S3 bucket permissions and "MFA on root account." Service outages live in AWS Health; software patches in Inspector / SSM Patch Manager; user-count is not a standard TA check.
AWS manages the underlying network infrastructure. VPC configuration, application code, and the application's traffic patterns are customer responsibilities.

Auto Scaling removes idle capacity; Reserved Instances trade commitment for steep discounts. Budgets only surface spend; IAM restrictions don't reduce existing instance cost; placing instances in a second Region adds cost.

Enterprise Support includes a designated Technical Account Manager and support for third-party software integration. Professional Services is a separate paid offering; partner-led support is a different program; the critical-incident response target is 15 minutes (not 5).
AWS Service Catalog lets administrators create curated portfolios of approved AWS products that end users can launch, restricting them to a vetted set of resources.
Customers patch the guest OS on their EC2 instances (including EC2-hosted databases). AWS patches DynamoDB, the hypervisor, and the OS for managed services like RDS.

The Well-Architected Framework explicitly recommends decoupling components (microservices, queues, events) and designing for elasticity. Consolidating accounts, buying hardware to host in AWS, and migrating physical network gear are anti-patterns.

Report DDoS attacks against AWS resources and AWS resources being used to host illegal/objectionable content to the AWS Abuse team. SQL injection from non-AWS IPs is a customer-side detection problem; internal policy violations and HTTP/HTTPS mismatches aren't abuse-team matters.
Patching guest OS and applications is the customer's obligation. Infrastructure patching, physical/environmental controls, and AWS infrastructure device configuration are AWS responsibilities.
Faster product launches and the ability to scale infrastructure on demand are the defining benefits of business agility on the cloud.

Quick capacity change (elasticity) and high economies of scale are stated AWS Cloud advantages. AWS doesn't manage customer infrastructure; deployment time decreases, not increases; fixed costs are replaced by variable costs.
Aggregating demand across many customers produces massive economies of scale that AWS passes back as lower per-unit pricing.
Step Functions, DynamoDB, and SNS are all serverless (no servers to provision). Options A, B, and D include EC2 or EMR, which require server management.
Patching Amazon EC2 (guest OS) is the customer's responsibility. Infrastructure patching, physical security, and network-infrastructure patching are AWS responsibilities.
Ensuring data is encrypted at rest is the customer's choice and obligation (customer enables encryption and manages keys). Disk wiping, firmware updates, and cabling specs are AWS responsibilities.

AWS Cloud's value proposition includes no long-term contracts — pay-as-you-go. Security is shared (not solely AWS "in" the cloud); new servers in minutes (not days); customers manage their own applications.

For an always-on, fixed-size workload running 1 year, Standard Reserved Instances give the deepest discount over On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed (unsuitable for an always-on DB); Convertible RIs trade discount for flexibility.

AWS upgrades the firmware of the underlying network infrastructure and maintains the physical security of edge locations. EC2 OS patching, IAM password rotation, and protecting root account access are customer duties.

Network ACLs are stateless and process rules in order, starting with the lowest-numbered rule, until one matches. They operate at the subnet level, not the instance level (that's security groups, which are stateful).
Running workloads effectively and continuously improving supporting processes is the description of the Operational Excellence pillar.
Elasticity is the ability to right-size and easily produce resources when needed. Instance restart time, max RAM, and pricing model are unrelated definitions.
Open a detailed AWS Support case for billing issues. Uploading data to S3, deploying pricing apps, or building a QuickSight dashboard don't engage the people who can fix billing problems.
Least privilege grants users the minimum access needed to complete their tasks. Time-limited ("current month") and job-title-based assignment are role-based, not least-privilege concepts.

Setting up S3 server-side encryption (the customer enables it and chooses keys) and configuring network/firewall rules are customer duties. RDS OS patching, physical data-center security, and compute capacity are AWS's.
AWS Cost Explorer provides graphical visualization of AWS spending and usage over time, plus monthly cost breakdowns. AWS Bills is itemized; CUR is raw data; Budgets is thresholds.
Enterprise Support is the only plan that includes a designated TAM, the Concierge team, and 24/7 technical support.
Encrypting application data at rest and ensuring AWS-user security training are customer duties. NTP servers, data-center physical access, and hardware disposal are AWS responsibilities.
Compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP) are downloaded on-demand from AWS Artifact.
AWS Config maintains an inventory and tracks configuration changes; CloudTrail records auditable logs of API calls. Both are core Reliability/operational-excellence change-management controls.

IAM roles (attached via an instance profile) let EC2 instances make AWS service calls with temporary credentials. Groups apply to users, policies are attached to identities, access keys belong to IAM users.
Client-side encryption and configuring Network ACLs are customer responsibilities. RDS OS patching, data-center staff training, and environmental controls are AWS responsibilities.
Loose coupling — components communicating via well-defined contracts so one failure doesn't cascade — is the cloud design principle that isolates failures between dependent components.
Amazon RDS and DynamoDB are managed database services. EBS is block storage, S3 is object storage, EFS is file storage.

Route 53 health-check / latency / failover routing and AWS Global Accelerator both work across Regions. ALB and NLB are regional load balancers; S3 Transfer Acceleration accelerates uploads, not failover.
AWS applies updates to the hypervisor and underlying infrastructure. Enabling S3 SSE, applying IAM policies, and patching the EC2 OS are customer duties.

AWS Artifact is the on-demand portal for the AWS SOC 2 report and other compliance documentation. Personal Health Dashboard is for service health; Trusted Advisor is best-practice checks; S3 is object storage.
Amazon Linux On-Demand EC2 instances bill per second (with a 60-second minimum). 3 hours, 5 minutes, 6 seconds bills exactly as 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds.
If Free Tier limits are exceeded or the 12-month period ends, AWS charges the standard pay-as-you-go rates for the overage. No retroactive billing, no frozen account, no contact required.
Amazon Macie uses ML to discover, classify, and monitor sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial data) in S3. Shield is DDoS protection, Network Firewall is L3/L4 filtering, Cognito is app user identity.
Physical and environmental controls are AWS-exclusive responsibilities. Guest OS patching, IAM password policy, and security awareness/training are customer (or shared) duties.

AWS Marketplace lets you both sell solutions to other AWS users and buy third-party software that runs on AWS. Spot Instances cannot be resold today, compliance documents live in AWS Artifact, and Snowball is ordered through the console.
Edge locations (used by CloudFront) deliver content closer to viewers and cache responses to reduce origin traffic. They are not for application hosting, NoSQL caches, or sending notifications.
Handling sudden 10× spikes is the textbook definition of scalability — the ability to grow capacity to match demand.
AWS Global Accelerator routes traffic to optimal regional endpoints over the AWS backbone, improving both availability (health-check based failover) and performance (lower-latency anycast IPs).
Managing VPC Network ACLs and encrypting data in transit and at rest are customer duties. EC2 hardware, failed disks, and AZ hardware deployment are AWS responsibilities.
AWS owns the network infrastructure and virtualization layer and physical security of hardware. Application data, guest OS, and customer credentials/policies are customer responsibilities.

Operating system administration is a TCO line item compared between on-prem (customer staffs and licenses) and AWS (largely covered by managed services). Project management and business analysis are not standard TCO comparison items.
AWS IAM Access Analyzer identifies resources (S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS keys, Lambda functions, SQS queues, Secrets Manager secrets) shared with external entities.
Amazon Aurora is the AWS managed relational engine that is MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible. Redshift is a data warehouse, DynamoDB is NoSQL, Neptune is a graph database.
AWS Snowball Edge can run a small set of Amazon EC2 instance types locally on the device for on-prem compute alongside data transfer. SMS, Aurora, and Trusted Advisor are not Snowball Edge features.

AWS lists three shared controls: Patch Management, Configuration Management, and Awareness & Training. RDS patching is AWS-only, physical/environmental are AWS-only, service/communications protection is customer.
Paying only for what you use and scaling resource usage to match demand are core to the Cost Optimization pillar.

Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployments automatically fail over to a standby in another AZ with the same endpoint, requiring no admin action. The other options misuse Route 53, ALBs, or NLBs in ways that don't preserve a database endpoint.

Storage hardware and physical servers are the canonical infrastructure cost lines compared between on-prem and AWS TCO. Software development, project management, and antivirus licenses are not infrastructure costs.

Enterprise Support has a 15-minute critical response target; Enterprise On-Ramp has 30 minutes — both meet "within 30 minutes." Business Support's critical target is 1 hour, which does not. Both Enterprise tiers also include consultative migration guidance.
AWS Cost Explorer includes a forecast feature that anticipates future AWS expenses based on historical usage.
S3 Versioning keeps every version of an object, so accidental overwrites and deletes can be recovered by restoring the prior version.
With DynamoDB the customer manages IAM permissions on tables and the choice of encryption options. AWS handles OS patching and infrastructure provisioning/maintenance.
Automatically recovering from failure is a stated design principle of the Reliability pillar.
Amazon DynamoDB is the AWS managed NoSQL key-value/document database. Aurora is relational, Redshift is a data warehouse, Neptune is a graph database.

Amazon SNS is a pub/sub service that can deliver messages over SMS, email, push, HTTP, SQS, and Lambda from a single topic. SES is email-only; CloudWatch alarms trigger but don't directly deliver SMS+email; SQS is queues.
High availability on AWS requires distributing across a minimum of two Availability Zones so the workload survives any single-AZ failure.
Firmware updates on hardware are AWS's obligation. Encryption in transit, OS patching of EC2, and encryption at rest are customer responsibilities.

Launching the additional instances in multiple AZs within a single Region increases availability — survives any single-AZ outage. Same-AZ defeats the purpose; cross-Region adds latency and complexity for a simple HA pattern.
Data-in-transit security and data-integrity authentication are customer responsibilities. Physical security, physical network devices, and storage device decommissioning are AWS responsibilities.

AWS Transit Gateway is the central hub that interconnects many VPCs (and on-prem networks) across Regions, replacing meshes of VPC peerings. Direct Connect is the physical circuit, Site-to-Site VPN is one-to-one over the internet, VPC endpoints connect a VPC to AWS services.
Amazon CloudWatch is the metrics + logs service used to monitor EC2 CPU and network utilization. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities, CloudTrail records API calls, Config tracks configuration.

Adopting managed services shifts OS patching to AWS, and AWS Systems Manager (Patch Manager) automates patching of customer-managed instances. AWS Support doesn't patch customer instances, Professional Services is paid consulting, license-included pricing doesn't reduce patching effort.

To withstand an environmental disruption (data center / power / cooling event), replicate across Availability Zones — each AZ is a discrete data center campus. Edge locations are caches, Regions are bigger units, Route 53 is DNS.
Loose coupling minimizes interdependence between components, letting them evolve and fail independently.
Changing the AWS Support plan and creating a CloudFront key pair both require the root user. EC2 key pairs and IAM group/permissions changes can be done by IAM users with the right permissions.
AWS CloudFormation provisions AWS resources from declarative templates — the canonical IaC service. Systems Manager is operations, CodeCommit is Git hosting, Config tracks configuration.
AWS Organizations centrally manages multiple accounts, applies SCPs, automates account creation, and provides consolidated billing across business units.

AWS Quick Start reference deployments are vetted CloudFormation templates that install popular technologies and architectures rapidly. CloudFront, CodeCommit, and Well-Architected docs don't deploy IT products.

CloudFront's global edge-location network caches frequently requested data near users, providing the lowest-latency response. CloudTrail is for audit logs, CloudFormation provisions resources, Direct Connect is a private network link.

On-demand compute capacity and enhanced security baseline are stated AWS Cloud benefits. AWS publishes service-specific SLAs (none 100%), code development isn't a primary Support benefit, and cost/complexity are reduced not increased.
On-Demand Instances handle short, no-interruption spikes without commitment — flexible and pay-as-you-go. Spot can be reclaimed; Reserved Instances/Dedicated Hosts require commitment.

AWS lists Patch Management and Cloud Awareness & Training as shared controls (along with Configuration Management). Physical/environmental and zone security are AWS-only; application data encryption is customer-only.
S3 Versioning is the feature that retains every object version, allowing recovery from accidental overwrites and deletes by shared users.
Deploying RDS across multiple AZs is a Reliability-pillar pattern — it provides automatic failover to a standby in another AZ when the primary fails.

The AWS Simple Monthly Calculator (today known as AWS Pricing Calculator) lets architects estimate the monthly cost of an architecture before deployment. Support cases aren't for cost estimates; manually summing prices is error-prone; TCO Calculator compares against on-prem, not estimates AWS-only spend.
Configuring firewalls and networks (security groups, NACLs) is the customer's duty. AWS secures the cloud infrastructure, patches RDS, and runs physical controls.
With Docker on EC2, AWS performs hardware maintenance in its facilities. The customer scales containers, schedules them, manages availability, and manages the guest OS.
"Stop guessing capacity" and increased speed-to-market are stated cloud benefits. Pricing is variable not fixed, upfront capex is reduced, physical data-center access is never provided.

AWS protects the network against IP spoofing and packet sniffing at the hypervisor/network layer, and AWS installs OS-level security patches on RDS (a managed service). Virus scans, app-to-ELB encryption choices, and security-group rules are customer duties.

AWS Outposts brings AWS infrastructure on-premises, hosting AWS services with minimum latency at a remote site where internet is poor and the app is latency/connectivity sensitive. Local Zones are AWS-managed near metro areas; Wavelength is for 5G edges.
AWS Marketplace is the online catalog for buying, deploying, and metering third-party software that runs on AWS.
Infrastructure recovery from a disaster aligns with the Reliability pillar — recovering from disruptions is one of its stated outcomes.

AWS Outposts (managed AWS rack on-prem) and Snowball Edge (compute + storage device) both let a business run AWS workloads exclusively on-premises. Pinpoint, WorkLink, and AppSync are not on-prem deployment vehicles.
A separate VPC provides an isolated virtual network within AWS. AZs are infrastructure, placement groups affect EC2 placement, Direct Connect is a private link to on-prem.
AWS aggregating demand across hundreds of thousands of customers produces operational efficiencies and economies of scale that are passed back to customers as periodic price reductions.
Amazon GuardDuty is an agentless, ML-driven threat detection service that monitors CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs — no software deployment required.
AWS Global Accelerator uses the AWS edge network (anycast IPs at edge locations) to route user traffic to regional endpoints. Aurora and Connect are regional; Outposts is on-prem.
IAM users have long-term access key IDs and secret access keys used for programmatic access (e.g., CLI). Roles provide temporary credentials, groups/policies have no credentials.
Edge location management is AWS-only. Application security is customer-only; patch management is shared; client-side data is customer-only.
AWS Regions are the geographic units that let customers deploy compute and storage globally. AZs are within a Region; tags and resource groups are organizational metadata.

"Automatically recover from failure" is a Reliability design principle. Single-AZ deployment is the opposite of reliable; cost optimization belongs to another pillar; ops-as-code is Operational Excellence.

Implementing controls to route application traffic (security groups, NACLs, route tables, load balancer config) is entirely the customer's responsibility. Infrastructure patching, physical/environmental controls, and underlying infrastructure are AWS responsibilities.
Agility is the AWS Cloud's ability to let users provision resources in minutes (vs. weeks on-prem) — enabling rapid experimentation and faster delivery.
Cost allocation tags let you slice AWS spend by any dimension you tag (team, project, environment), giving fine-grained expenditure tracking in Cost Explorer and the CUR.

AWS Fargate is the serverless compute engine for containers — used with ECS and EKS, it eliminates the need to provision or manage EC2 hosts. FSx is file storage, ECS without Fargate runs on EC2, EC2 is exactly the thing we're avoiding.

AWS Elastic Beanstalk's enhanced health monitoring automatically watches the deployed application and underlying resources, reporting health to the console. API Gateway monitors itself; Lambda doesn't watch other apps; Config tracks resource configuration.
"Anticipate failure" is a stated design principle of Operational Excellence. Make small, reversible changes; automate operations; refine procedures often — not the reverse.

Data center security is a TCO line that AWS provides (built into service prices) and on-prem customers pay for separately. Project management, antivirus licenses, and software development aren't AWS-vs-on-prem TCO comparison items.
AWS IAM Access Analyzer reviews access policies and produces findings + actionable recommendations to make them secure and least-privilege.
Testing recovery procedures regularly is a Reliability design principle. Vertical scaling, manual recovery, and manual infrastructure changes are anti-patterns.
AWS Trusted Advisor has a security check that flags S3 buckets with public read/write permissions, helping identify publicly accessible buckets quickly.
Elasticity (scale up for seasonal peaks, down at quiet times) and pay-as-you-go pricing (no fixed commitment) directly address unpredictable seasonal demand.

Testing recovery procedures and automatically recovering from failure are both Reliability design principles. Monolithic architecture and "measuring overall efficiency" don't improve reliability; consumption model is Cost Optimization.
Amazon EFS and Amazon FSx are managed file storage services (NFS for EFS, Windows/Lustre/OpenZFS/NetApp for FSx variants). S3 is object, EBS is block, instance store is ephemeral block.
AWS Artifact is the on-demand, self-service portal for AWS compliance control reports (SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP).

AWS Trusted Advisor performs automated evaluations across five categories; AWS Professional Services provides human expertise. Both help evaluate cloud-based applications. Systems Manager is for ops, APN partners help but aren't "AWS," Secrets Manager is unrelated.
AWS Organizations centralizes management of many AWS accounts, including consolidated billing and SCP-based cost/spend controls.
AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated private network connection between on-prem and AWS — lower jitter, higher bandwidth than internet VPN.
Elasticity solves under-utilization: in the cloud you release resources you aren't using rather than over-provisioning and idling.
Amazon RDS managed advantage vs. self-managed DBs: AWS handles OS maintenance (and engine patching, backups, hardware). The customer still manages data and DB engine selection.
Service control policies (SCPs) define the maximum permissions for accounts in AWS Organizations. They don't manage AZs, Regions, or edge locations.

AWS KMS creates and manages cryptographic keys used to encrypt data at rest in S3, EBS, RDS, and other services. GuardDuty detects threats, Shield protects against DDoS, Security Hub aggregates findings.
Spot Instances offer the largest discounts (up to ~90%) for workloads that can tolerate interruption — perfect for a seldom-run data analysis job that can be stopped without damage.

Consolidated billing aggregates usage so accounts reach volume-discount tiers sooner and produces one bill for many accounts. There is no extra fee; installment payments aren't part of it; custom budgets are AWS Budgets.
Dedicated Hosts expose the physical sockets/cores/VM count to the customer, enabling Bring-Your-Own-License (BYOL) for per-socket/core/VM-licensed Windows software.
Amazon CloudFront caches content at edge locations close to users worldwide, reducing latency for a global audience.

The Cost and Usage Report provides detailed cost data; billing alerts via CloudWatch alarms notify when spend crosses thresholds. Product pages, Pricing Calculator, and Price List API are reference data, not monitoring.
Amazon EFS is a managed file storage service. Redshift, RDS, and VPC are not generic data storage services in this question's framing.
AWS CodeDeploy automates the delivery of application changes to EC2, on-prem, and Lambda. AppFlow moves data between SaaS apps, PrivateLink is networking, EKS Distro is a Kubernetes distribution.
AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service — no servers to manage. EC2 is virtual machines, DynamoDB is a database, CodeCommit is Git.
Elastic Load Balancing distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets in multiple Availability Zones within a Region. It is not multi-Region by itself.
AWS Storage Gateway connects on-premises storage to the AWS Cloud (File, Volume, and Tape Gateways) for hybrid storage.

To anticipate costs for a new application before deployment, use the AWS Simple Monthly Calculator (now AWS Pricing Calculator). Cost Explorer/CUR work on actual usage; Backtrack is an Aurora feature.
Fault tolerance is the built-in redundancy of an application's components so the workload survives component failure. Growth without redesign is scalability; data restore is recoverability.
Amazon Athena lets you run standard SQL queries directly against data stored in S3 — no infrastructure to manage. Glue is ETL, Data Pipeline is workflow, CloudSearch is search.
Agility — provisioning resources in minutes and easily changing them as requirements evolve — fits a startup that must launch fast and may need to pivot.
On-demand compute and enhanced security are cloud advantages. AWS doesn't offer a blanket 100% SLA, code dev support isn't a defining cloud benefit, and cost/complexity decrease — not increase.
Amazon Elastic Transcoder converts media files between formats suitable for phones, tablets, and browsers. Comprehend is NLP, Glue is ETL, Rekognition is image/video analysis.
Consolidated billing (an AWS Organizations feature) gives one invoice across many member accounts while still letting each account's costs be tracked individually.
"Stop guessing capacity" plus paying only for resources consumed is the Pay-As-You-Go pricing advantage.

AWS CloudTrail records all account API activity including Management Console actions, providing the audit trail of user/account changes. SNS notifies, VPC Flow Logs are network traffic, CloudHSM is key storage.
Installing security patches on EC2 Linux instances and choosing to encrypt data at rest on S3 are customer duties. Physical host access, EBS device wiping, and RDS patching are AWS responsibilities.

The IAM account password policy lets administrators set minimum length, character requirements, rotation, and reuse rules for IAM user passwords. User policies grant permissions, SCPs define org-level limits, Security Hub aggregates findings.
AWS Trusted Advisor has a Security check that flags security groups with overly permissive rules (0.0.0.0/0). CloudWatch is metrics, VPC Flow Logs is traffic, CloudTrail is API audit.
AWS KMS is the managed service for creating, controlling, and auditing encryption keys for data. It does not create IAM access keys or MFA tokens.

AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager automates patching of self-managed databases on EC2 on a schedule. RDS-console automatic patching only applies to RDS-managed instances; Config tracks configuration; manual SSH per instance does not scale.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically launches and terminates the required number of instances based on demand and policies. ELB distributes traffic but doesn't change capacity.
Least privilege grants only the permissions required to perform a given task — the minimum needed and no more.
Building an RDS Multi-AZ instance assumes a single AZ may fail and provides automatic failover to a standby — "design for failure" applied to the database tier.
Managing application software updates is the customer's responsibility. AWS owns physical hardware, hypervisor, and data center access.
AWS Regions consist of multiple Availability Zones. AZs don't span Regions, a VPC is regional (subnets must live in one Region), and a subnet lives in exactly one AZ.
AWS Direct Connect provides a private (not internet) network connection between an on-premises corporate network and AWS. VPC peering connects VPCs; Route 53 is DNS; Connect is a contact center.
IAM policies grant or restrict user/role access to S3 buckets and other resources. Key pairs are for SSH, Inspector scans vulnerabilities, security groups are network-level firewalls.
"Learn from operational failures" (and continuously improve) is a stated Well-Architected design principle. Large infrequent changes, manual configuration, and monolithic design are anti-patterns.
Amazon Route 53 health checks detect endpoint failures and reroute DNS to healthy targets, providing failover for a website server outage.

With the Developer Support plan, the developer opens a support case in the AWS Support Center. Professional Services is paid consulting; TAMs and Consulting Partners are not included in Developer Support.

AWS X-Ray traces requests as they travel through a distributed application, surfacing latency, errors, and dependencies end-to-end. Cloud9 is an IDE, CodeStar is project setup, Cloud Map is service discovery.
Customers configure their own applications and security groups. Facilities access, hardware lifecycle, and network infrastructure protection are AWS responsibilities.

AWS Trusted Advisor's Performance category surfaces optimization tips (idle resources, oversized instances, S3 best practices). Inspector finds vulnerabilities; CloudWatch is metrics; CloudTrail is API logs.
Outbound data transfers (egress) and compute in active use both incur cost. Unused Lambda functions and S3 buckets themselves are free; inbound data transfer to AWS is free.
AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes over time and can invoke automated remediation when resources drift out of compliance with rules.
Amazon Glacier (now S3 Glacier storage classes) is the lowest-cost option for long-term archival backups. RDS is a DB; Snowball is bulk data transfer; EBS is high-cost block storage.

Readily available resources with minute-scale provisioning and scalable compute capacity are the textbook speed-and-agility benefits. Free Tier, physical access, and lower cost address different dimensions.
Scalability — matching provisioned capacity to current demand — is the cloud advantage being described. Reliability is about uptime; global reach is geographic; HA is fault tolerance.
S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on observed access patterns, saving cost on infrequently accessed data without retrieval fees.

A Site-to-Site VPN (over the internet) and AWS Direct Connect (private circuit) are the two standard ways to connect a VPC to on-premises resources. The other options don't establish hybrid network connectivity.
AWS Transit Gateway is the hub that interconnects an on-premises network to many VPCs across many Regions through a single point of management.
Multiple AWS Regions is a defining feature of AWS Global Infrastructure. Agility, elasticity, and pay-as-you-go are unrelated benefit names.
AWS OpsWorks provides managed instances of Chef and Puppet for configuration management. CloudFormation is IaC, Config is tracking, Systems Manager is operations.
"Stop guessing capacity" plus paying only when resources are used is the Pay-As-You-Go pricing model.

AWS WAF lets you define rules (including managed rule groups) to filter and automatically block SQL injection and other Layer-7 attacks. Security groups, ELB, and NACLs operate at L3/L4 and don't inspect HTTP payloads.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling makes EC2 fleets elastic by adding and removing instances based on demand.
Amazon Rekognition automatically detects objects, scenes, and faces in images and video — saving the time of doing it manually or with Mechanical Turk.

AWS Snowball physically ships ruggedized appliances to move petabytes of on-prem data into AWS far faster than the internet. Global Accelerator is anycast traffic routing; Transfer Acceleration helps S3 uploads up to TB; Connect is a contact center.
AWS Marketplace is the online catalog where customers can find, test, buy, and deploy software that runs on AWS. Lumberyard was a game engine, Artifact is compliance reports, CloudSearch is search.
A multi-Region active-active architecture serves users from the closest Region with low latency and survives any one Region failing. Single-Region designs incur higher latency for distant users.
Updating the guest OS on EC2 is the customer's responsibility. AWS manages Lambda infrastructure, DynamoDB internals, and S3 infrastructure.
To survive a widespread natural disaster, deploy across multiple AWS Regions. Multi-AZ within one Region won't survive a regional disaster; AWS Artifact is for compliance docs not app artifacts.
S3 + Lambda + DynamoDB is a classic decoupled, serverless, scalable pattern — each component scales independently. The other options describe single-instance or monolithic designs.
Consolidated billing in AWS Organizations aggregates usage across member accounts so volume discounts apply across the whole org — no need to move resources or commit to RIs upfront.
AWS's global footprint (Regions and edge locations worldwide) lets customers keep data and compute close to their consumers, reducing latency and meeting data residency needs.
Everything from the virtualization layer down to physical facility security is solely AWS's responsibility — "security of the cloud."
When AWS managed policies don't grant required rights, create a custom (customer-managed) IAM policy with the needed permissions. Shield, WAF, and KMS don't grant IAM rights.
AWS Systems Manager Session Manager provides browser-based shell access to EC2 without opening inbound SSH ports or managing SSH keys — far better posture than open SSH.
Spot Instances fit infrequent, interruptible workloads at up to ~90% discount. An always-on website (A), 99.999% SLA workloads (B), and a critical legacy DB (C) shouldn't run on Spot.
Amazon SNS is the AWS pub/sub push service for delivering time-sensitive notifications (SMS, email, mobile push, HTTP) to many subscribers. Kinesis is streaming, MQ/SQS are queues (pull).

Changing the AWS Support plan and closing the AWS account both require root user credentials. EC2 instance type changes, Systems Manager resource grouping, and EKS app operations are IAM-permitted tasks.
Dynamically and predictively scaling to meet usage demand is the cloud-native pattern that exploits flexibility and agility. Static provisioning and physical data-center access are anti-patterns.
Amazon Route 53 can register DNS domains as well as host authoritative DNS. Encryption in transit is ACM/TLS; SSL certificates are ACM; dedicated connections are Direct Connect.
VPC peering connects two VPCs (same or different Region) for private routing. VPC endpoints reach AWS services; ClassicLink is deprecated; Direct Connect is for on-prem.

An IAM role attached to the EC2 instance (via an instance profile) provides temporary credentials so the app can call SNS without embedding access keys. ACM is for TLS, Security Hub aggregates findings, GuardDuty detects threats.
Scaling compute capacity up and down in response to demand is elasticity. Agility is about deployment speed; global deployments concern reach; cost savings is an outcome but not the named advantage.
Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL database delivering single-digit-millisecond performance. Redshift is a data warehouse, RDS is relational, S3 is object storage.

AWS Partner Network Consulting Partners help customers design and build on AWS when in-house expertise is limited. Technology Partners sell software; Marketplace is a catalog; Service Catalog distributes approved products.
AWS Control Tower automates the setup of a secure, multi-account AWS environment (landing zone) with governance guardrails.
Closing an AWS account requires the root user. Creating log files, modifying IAM user permissions, and deleting IAM users can all be done by appropriately permissioned IAM users.
AWS Fargate is the serverless engine for containers — AWS manages cluster size, scheduling, and the environment, so the customer no longer manages Docker hosts themselves.
Auto-scaling EC2 by traffic and resizing RDS as needs change are both expressions of elasticity. Traffic routing is load balancing, compliance docs are unrelated, IaC is automation/IaC.

No upfront commitments and on-demand resource provisioning are stated cloud advantages. AWS doesn't manage all security (shared model), there's no free unlimited storage, and customers don't get physical infrastructure control.

AWS manages cloud-infrastructure maintenance and capacity planning for physical servers. Application security and development remain customer responsibilities; cost planning is the customer's decision.
AWS CloudFormation defines infrastructure as code (declarative YAML/JSON templates). CodePipeline is CI/CD orchestration, CodeDeploy applies code releases, Direct Connect is networking.

Building the application to scale automatically (up for demand, down to save cost) is how AWS helps manage costs under unpredictable usage. AWS doesn't refund on resize; Spot isn't automatic for everything; CloudWatch doesn't predict capacity by itself.

Multi-AZ EC2 deployments prevent a single point of failure and increase availability. Cost typically rises slightly (more instances), cross-Region latency isn't addressed, and load is not increased — it's spread.

AWS Outposts extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to on-premises facilities for a true hybrid experience. Snowmobile is data transfer, Local Zones extend metros, Fargate is serverless containers.
AWS Storage Gateway's Tape Gateway integrates with existing tape libraries and backs the data to S3/Glacier transparently. EBS, S3, and EFS don't speak the iSCSI VTL protocol.
Rightsize before migration (so you start cloud usage efficient) and continuously after onboarding (workloads change). Rightsizing only at peaks or after RI purchases is too late and locks in waste.

AWS Budgets supports RI/Savings-Plans utilization alerts — you can be notified when RI utilization drops below a target. Budgets alerts but doesn't prevent resource creation or split bills across payment methods.
Elasticity (scale for seasonal peaks, down at quiet times) and pay-as-you-go pricing (no fixed commitment) directly address unpredictable seasonal demand.
Consolidated billing produces combined-usage volume discounts across all member accounts (S3, data transfer, etc.). It doesn't change Personal Health Dashboard access, security posture, or IAM.
AWS Direct Connect provides a private, low-latency, dedicated network link from an on-prem location to AWS. Internet VPN has higher jitter; VPC peering is VPC-to-VPC.
Amazon Polly converts text to lifelike speech in dozens of voices and languages. Kendra is enterprise search, Rekognition is image/video, Connect is contact center.
Spot Instance pricing fluctuates with EC2 supply and demand — customers pay the current Spot price (subject to a cap). On-Demand and Reserved pricing are static.

AWS Organizations centrally manages and integrates multiple AWS accounts with consolidated billing, SCPs, and account governance. IAM handles users within one account, SCT is for DB migration, Config is for configuration tracking.

VPC Flow Logs capture metadata about inbound and outbound IP traffic in a VPC and publish it to CloudWatch Logs or S3. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities; endpoints are network access; NAT gateways are SNAT.
Email/web cases via the AWS Support Center are available on every plan. Live chat and phone are reserved for Business+ plans; there is no local AWS Support office or video conference for support.
AWS Budgets lets you set custom spend or usage thresholds and triggers email/SNS alerts on actual or forecasted overage.
For managed services, AWS handles database backups and installing/maintaining the operating system. Customers still configure OS firewalls (on EC2), data access controls, and database user accounts.

Cost-estimation tools include the Detailed Billing Report (historical), AWS Simple Monthly Calculator / Pricing Calculator (pre-deployment), and the TCO Calculator (vs. on-prem). Cost allocation tags slice existing spend but don't estimate; there is no service called "Cost Estimator."

Two AZs in the same Region put the instances in separate data centers with low-latency private connectivity between them. Cross-Region adds latency; on-prem + AWS is hybrid; placement groups affect networking but not data-center separation.
AWS replaces large upfront capital expenses with lower variable operating expenses — both lower variable and lower upfront.

Intrusion attempts originating from AWS IP addresses should be reported to the AWS Abuse team. AZ disruptions are surfaced via Service Health Dashboard; access trouble and payment changes go to Support.

Distributing across Availability Zones is the AWS feature that delivers high availability — survives any single AZ failure. Direct Connect is connectivity, data centers aren't customer-controlled, VPC is a network boundary.

Use IAM users for individuals who need long-lived credentials and to add to IAM groups for permissions inheritance. Apps on EC2 should use IAM roles; mobile apps should use Cognito or role temporary credentials; SSO is handled by IAM Identity Center.
Attach an IAM policy to an IAM group and add the users — every group member inherits the permissions. Policies on individual users don't scale; Cognito user pools are app-level identity.
AWS KMS provides the encryption keys used to encrypt EBS volumes. ACM is for TLS certificates, Systems Manager is operations, Config is configuration tracking.

AWS Partner Network Technology Partners are ISVs and management/security vendors whose products run on or integrate with AWS. TAMs, Consulting Partners, and Concierge are service offerings, not solution providers.
AWS Elastic Beanstalk and AWS OpsWorks are application-deployment services. Config is configuration tracking, Application Discovery Service is for migration assessment, Kinesis is streaming.
Amazon CloudFront (CDN) and AWS Global Accelerator both leverage AWS's global edge-location network. Fargate is serverless containers, Wavelength is 5G edge, VPC is a virtual network.
With EC2 the customer manages file-system encryption and guest OS firewall (iptables, Windows firewall) configuration. Hardware, hypervisor patching, and physical security are AWS responsibilities.

AWS Outposts brings AWS compute, storage, and APIs on-prem to factories, supporting low-latency local apps with system interdependencies. Greengrass is for IoT device fleets; Snowball Edge is for transient compute/storage.
Amazon Neptune is the managed graph database, ideal for social-network style data and graph queries. RDS/Aurora are relational, DynamoDB is key-value, Redshift is analytical.
Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL key-value/document database. Aurora and RDS are relational; Redshift is a data warehouse.
Security groups are stateful, instance-level firewalls. Network ACLs operate at the subnet level (stateless); Trusted Advisor is checks; virtual private gateways connect VPN.
Cost allocation tags applied to resources let you break out spend by application in Cost Explorer and the CUR — even when everything lives in a single AWS account.

To handle doubling data each month within a fixed wall-clock window, distribute the workload across multiple EC2 instances and run in parallel (horizontal scaling). A bigger or different instance type alone has limits; bare metal doesn't change the parallelism story.

All Upfront Reserved Instances give the deepest discount for a workload that runs continuously for a year and cannot tolerate interruption. Partial Upfront is next; Dedicated Instances are pricier; On-Demand is full price.

Trusted Advisor provides cost-optimization recommendations and flags security findings caused by permissions. It does not scan applications for software vulnerabilities (Inspector does), apply auto-remediation, or alert on instance compromise (GuardDuty does).
Amazon CloudWatch collects CPU utilization metrics for EC2 instances. Config tracks configuration; CloudTrail tracks API calls; Inspector finds vulnerabilities.
AWS Organizations lets a master payer account aggregate billing from member accounts.
Moving to AWS replaces multi-month procurement cycles with on-demand access to IT resources — the agility advantage of the cloud.

AWS Shield (Standard at no extra cost; Advanced as a paid tier) protects AWS resources against DDoS attacks. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities, GuardDuty detects threats, Detective investigates findings.
AWS Organizations aggregates billing across many AWS accounts (consolidated billing). QuickSight is BI, Budgets sets spend alerts, Forecast is ML forecasting.
Elasticity (scale with demand) and Agility (provision in minutes) are stated cloud advantages. No service has "unlimited uptime," colocation is on-prem, and AWS replaces capex with opex.
For non-stop workloads (steady-state always running), Reserved Instances deliver the deepest discount over On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed; Dedicated is pricier; On-Demand has no commitment.
AWS CodeCommit is the managed Git source control service whose primary purpose is software version control. CodeStar is project setup, the CLI is a tool, Cognito is identity.
AWS Artifact is the on-demand self-service portal for compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP). The Abuse team handles security incidents; Support handles tickets; Config tracks configuration.
"Enable traceability" — monitor, alert, and audit changes in real time — is a Security pillar design principle. AWS Config recording configuration history maps to this principle.
Loose coupling is a stated AWS Cloud design principle. Single points of failure, monolithic design, and exclusive vertical scaling are anti-patterns.
AWS's massive economies of scale — aggregated demand across many customers — drive the continuous reductions in cloud pricing.

Agility is the cloud's ability to reduce the time required to make IT resources available to developers — supporting faster innovation. The other options describe pay-as-you-go, capacity planning, and global reach.
Network ACLs operate at the subnet level inside a VPC as stateless firewalls. Security groups are at the instance level. Traffic Mirroring captures packets; IGW provides internet egress.

Amazon WorkSpaces provides managed Windows virtual desktops; AWS Site-to-Site VPN provides secure network connections from remote workers/sites. AppStream 2.0 streams apps not desktops; Connect is contact center; ECS is containers.

IAM is a shared responsibility: AWS provides the service and infrastructure, the customer configures users, groups, roles, and policies. SSE setup is customer; firewall configuration is customer; physical hardware is AWS-only.
Amazon S3 static website hosting serves HTML/CSS/JS at very low cost. Glacier is for archives, DynamoDB is a database, EFS is shared file storage.
Amazon SQS message queues let producers and consumers run independently — the canonical decoupling primitive. Config is configuration tracking, Batch runs batch jobs, SES is email.
Amazon Route 53 (DNS) and Amazon CloudFront (CDN) are global services. EC2, S3, and DynamoDB are regional services (S3 buckets and DynamoDB tables live in a Region).

Decoupling components — isolating them so one failure doesn't cascade — directly addresses the described tightly-coupled-failure problem. Elasticity and parallelism don't fix coupling; doubling resources can't survive coupled-component failure.

Amazon Macie uses ML to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial) stored in Amazon S3. Inspector is vulnerability scanning; GuardDuty is threat detection; Secrets Manager stores secrets.

Create an IAM role with the required permissions and attach it to the EC2 instance via an instance profile — temporary, automatically rotated credentials, no embedded keys. Hard-coded user keys are an anti-pattern.
Trusted Advisor's 5 categories: Cost Optimization, Performance, Security, Fault Tolerance, Service Limits. Of the options, Cost optimization and Security match.
Backup and restore is the least expensive of the AWS DR strategies — store backups only; restore on demand. Pilot light, Warm standby, and Multi-site cost progressively more.
Amazon EC2 Instance Store is ephemeral — data is lost when the instance stops or terminates. EBS, EFS, and S3 all persist independently of instance lifecycle.
Amazon FSx for Windows File Server provides fully managed Windows file storage accessible via the SMB protocol with Active Directory integration. EFS is NFS-only; S3 is object; EBS is block.
AWS Regions are core components of the AWS Global Infrastructure (along with Availability Zones and edge locations). Alexa, Lightsail, and Organizations aren't infrastructure components.
An internet gateway allows the VPC to communicate with the public internet (in and out). It does not throttle bandwidth, terminate VPNs, or load balance.
AWS Artifact provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance reports (SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP).
AWS Pricing Calculator estimates costs before deployment. Cost Explorer / CUR analyze historical spend; Trusted Advisor checks best practices.
EC2 Auto Scaling groups automatically add or replace instances across multiple Availability Zones to maintain capacity and recover from instance/AZ failures, increasing HA.
Stateless, interruptible, massively parallel workloads are the textbook fit for Spot Instances (up to ~90% off On-Demand).
MFA on accounts/users and Trusted Advisor security checks both materially improve AWS security posture. Artifact is documentation; broad permissions weaken security; Cloud9 is an IDE.

AWS Organizations centrally manages billing and applies controlled-access governance (SCPs, OUs) across multiple AWS accounts. IAM handles users within one account; Cost Explorer/Budgets are billing analytics.
Patching and securing the guest operating system on EC2 is the customer's responsibility under the shared model.
High availability means the application remains accessible even if an underlying resource fails. The other options describe support, pricing, and global reach.
Subnets and internet gateways are VPC resources. Objects/ACLs and buckets are S3, groups/roles are IAM, access policies span multiple services.
Elasticity (scale to match demand) and Pay-as-you-go pricing (only pay for what you use) make AWS cost-viable for fluctuating workloads.
Over 100 Mbps internet, 40 TB would take roughly a month to transfer. AWS Snowball ships physical devices and delivers in days — faster and cheaper for large bulk migrations.
The AWS CLI provides programmatic access to AWS services from the command line. Trusted Advisor is checks; CodeDeploy is deployment; the Management Console is a UI.

AWS Outposts brings AWS infrastructure on-premises so data can remain local (regulatory) while still using AWS services and APIs with low latency. Local Zones are AWS-managed near metros, not on-prem; Availability Zones aren't customer hostable.
AWS Pricing Calculator generates approximate dollar-amount cost estimates for proposed AWS architectures before deployment.
For a steady-state self-managed Oracle DB on EC2 over 3 years, Reserved Instances (especially All-Upfront) maximize savings vs On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed; Dedicated Instances are pricier.

Amazon CloudFront's global edge network is the most cost-effective way to distribute photos and videos worldwide with low latency. S3 cross-region replication moves data but doesn't deliver at low latency; VPN/PrivateLink are wrong tools.
The AWS Knowledge Center publishes answers to frequently asked billing and account questions. Marketplace sells products, Pinpoint is messaging, Connect is contact center.
AWS Direct Connect (private network link) and AWS Storage Gateway (hybrid storage) extend on-premises infrastructure into the AWS Cloud. EBS, CloudFront, and Connect don't serve this purpose.
Amazon CloudWatch supports billing alarms — triggered when estimated charges exceed a threshold. Config, X-Ray, and CloudTrail don't generate billing-based warnings.

AWS CloudTrail records who signed in to the Management Console and when — including the last-access timestamp. Cognito is app user identity; Inspector scans vulnerabilities; GuardDuty detects threats.
AWS CodePipeline is a CI/CD service that automates the build, test, and deploy stages of application delivery. AppSync is GraphQL APIs; Batch runs batch jobs; DataSync moves data.
Amazon Aurora's storage is replicated six ways across three AZs by default — highly available out of the box. RDS Multi-AZ requires opt-in; EC2 and NAT instances are single by default.

AWS Storage Gateway provides hybrid storage with elastic and efficient connectivity between on-premises and AWS storage. Glacier is archival, AWS Backup orchestrates managed backups, EFS is shared file storage in AWS.
Amazon Comprehend is the managed NLP service that performs sentiment analysis on text. Textract extracts text from documents, Translate translates languages, Rekognition analyzes images/video.
Amazon S3 offers virtually unlimited storage capacity (5 TB is the maximum object size, not the total). Buckets can hold an unlimited number of objects.
The AWS Concierge Support team is exclusively available to Enterprise Support customers. Trusted Advisor and Support cases are available on multiple tiers; Connect is unrelated.

AWS Security Bulletins are the official channel for AWS to publish security advisories and announcements. Personal Health Dashboard is about service health; Secrets Manager stores secrets; Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.
Amazon CloudFront (CDN at edge) and AWS Global Accelerator (anycast at edge) both lower global latency. VPN and Direct Connect are for specific endpoints; Glacier is archival storage.
Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL database (key-value and document). Aurora and RDS are relational; Redshift is a data warehouse; Neptune is a graph DB.
Separate accounts for each department (under one organization for billing) and cost allocation tags on resources are the two standard ways to break out AWS spend by department.
AWS CloudFormation (IaC templates) and AWS OpsWorks (Chef/Puppet-based config management) both automate infrastructure. CloudFront is CDN; Batch runs jobs; QuickSight is BI.
AWS Pricing Calculator generates cost estimates for proposed deployments before resources are launched. Cost Explorer/CUR/Budgets work on actual spend.

60 TB in 10 days from on-prem to S3 calls for AWS Snowball — physical appliances ship in days. DMS migrates databases, Direct Connect is a network link (still slow at that volume), Glacier is archival storage.
Amazon DynamoDB is a key-value (and document) NoSQL database.
AWS WAF lets you create custom rules (and use managed rule groups) to block SQL injection and other application-layer attacks. Security groups, NACLs, and Shield operate at L3/L4, not L7.
AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN over internet) and AWS Direct Connect (private dedicated circuit) connect AWS Cloud resources to on-premises networks.

On-Demand Instances activate when needed, stay active as long as required, and bill per second — perfect when usage is intermittent but the instance must remain on for a procedure. Spot can be interrupted; RIs/Dedicated are commitment-based.
AWS Pricing Calculator estimates AWS costs for a proposed hosting plan before the workload is migrated. Budgets/Cost Explorer/Trusted Advisor analyze existing usage.
Enabling MFA and configuring a strong IAM password policy directly strengthen account login security. ACM is for TLS certificates; Cognito is for app users; Organizations is multi-account governance.
Splitting one account into many AWS accounts provides administrative isolation between workloads (blast radius) and reduces the risk that compromise of one account exposes everything else.
AWS Organizations consolidates usage across member accounts so the org qualifies for tiered volume discounts that no single account could reach alone.

AWS WAF protects Amazon CloudFront, Amazon API Gateway, Application Load Balancers, and AWS AppSync. Of the listed options, CloudFront and API Gateway qualify. Route 53, Transfer Family, and Site-to-Site VPN are not WAF-protected.
Automatically recovering unhealthy applications via ASG + ALB is the Reliability pillar applied in practice — recovering from failure without manual intervention.

Dedicated Hosts let you Bring Your Own License (BYOL) for software licensed by physical cores/sockets/VMs. Default tenancy, On-Demand Capacity Reservations, and Dedicated Reserved Instances don't expose the physical core count for BYOL purposes.
Amazon S3 offers virtually unlimited highly durable (11 9s) object storage. Redshift is a data warehouse, EFS is file, ECS is containers.
Moving on-prem workloads to AWS eliminates ongoing data-center run and maintenance expenses (power, cooling, real estate, hardware refresh). The other options overstate AWS's role.

Of the listed services, Amazon S3 (object storage commonly described as storing files) and AWS Storage Gateway (which includes a File Gateway for NFS/SMB access) are the most file-oriented choices. Lambda and SageMaker aren't storage; EBS is block storage.
Amazon CloudFront caches video content at the global edge network for low-latency streaming with high data transfer rates over the AWS backbone.
"Global reach" describes AWS's ability to deploy infrastructure across many geographic Regions interconnected by AWS's private low-latency backbone.
AWS Organizations consolidates usage across accounts, qualifying the organization for AWS's tiered volume discounts. Savings Plans and Pricing Calculator are different mechanisms.
IAM Groups link multiple users to a shared set of permissions — attach a policy to the group and every member inherits it.
Amazon CloudFront caches images at the global edge network for low-latency viewing worldwide and is the most cost-effective option for serving global media.
Amazon SQS provides message queues that decouple producers from consumers — applications can scale and fail independently.
EC2 web servers in AWS calling out to a traditional on-premises application is a hybrid architecture by definition.
"Anticipate failure" is a stated design principle of the Operational Excellence pillar. Documentation is supportive but not the named principle; performance and cost belong to other pillars.

Amazon WorkSpaces virtual Windows desktops are customer-managed at the OS level — the customer is responsible for keeping the desktop OS updated and patched. FSx, Directory Service, and RDS are managed by AWS at the OS level.

AWS CloudTrail records the API call that terminated the EC2 instance — including the caller, time, and source IP — so you can investigate the unavailability. CloudWatch Logs may have related logs but doesn't directly identify the API action.
Amazon S3 Glacier is the lowest-cost option for long-term retention (7 years). S3 Standard is far pricier; Snowball is transfer; Redshift is analytics.
A Well-Architected review helps identify design gaps and evaluate design decisions against best practices. It is not mandatory, not part of SLAs, and does not eliminate ongoing audits.
Auto-recovering unhealthy instances with ASG + ALB is a Reliability pillar pattern (recover from failure).
Updating firmware on the underlying EC2 host hardware is AWS's responsibility. NACL/security group rules and EC2 OS patching are customer responsibilities.
AWS CloudFormation models and provisions AWS resources from declarative templates — that's its core purpose. It doesn't update application code, set up data lakes, or produce billing reports.
Withstanding failures with minimal downtime is the definition of high availability. Agility is deployment speed, elasticity is matching capacity to demand, scalability is growth without redesign.
With AWS Lambda the customer creates and versions function code. AWS handles servers/OS, scaling, and runtime updates.

The AWS Cost and Usage Report provides the most granular spend data — by product, tag, account, hour/day/month. RI and SP utilization reports cover purchasing, Budgets reports show threshold tracking.
Create an IAM user with programmatic access only (no console password) and attach an IAM policy granting only Amazon RDS access. This combines least privilege with the CLI/SDK-only requirement.
Designing for automated recovery from failure is a core Well-Architected design philosophy. Provisioning for peak, tight coupling, and ignoring data locality are anti-patterns.

AWS X-Ray traces user requests as they travel through distributed (including serverless) application components, surfacing latency and errors. CloudTrail is API audit, CloudWatch is metrics/logs, Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.
Amazon Redshift is the petabyte-scale managed data warehouse on AWS. DynamoDB is NoSQL OLTP; RDS is relational OLTP; ElastiCache is in-memory.

Replacing capital expenses with variable expenses, and increased business agility, are immediate AWS Cloud benefits. AWS reduces IT staff burden, customers don't control physical infra, and security is shared (not solely AWS).

AWS reduces both Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) and operating expenditure (opex) for typical workloads. AWS does not increase capex; deferred payment plans and credit lines aren't AWS economic advantages.
AWS IAM is an "always free" service — there is no charge for IAM itself. S3, ELB, and WAF all have usage-based charges.

Configuring a strong password policy and rotating access keys regularly are stated IAM best practices. Sharing credentials, using access keys for console login, and avoiding roles are all anti-patterns.

AWS CloudFormation lets a business build reusable templates that deploy AWS resources in a controlled, repeatable way. Marketplace sells software, AMI is one instance image, OpsWorks is Chef/Puppet config management.
AWS lowers TCO by minimizing large up-front capital expenditures (no buying servers/network gear); customers still have operational expenses and third-party license costs.
Amazon CloudFront's global edge network caches content close to viewers, guaranteeing low latency for global users of an EC2-hosted website. Route 53 is DNS; ELB is regional; Lambda is compute.
